assertions.description: Output is inline HTML — no markdown
assertions.check: response contains <style> and <div or <table but zero markdown table syntax (|---|)

assertions.description: Both companies have their own column in the scorecard
assertions.check: scorecard table contains NVDA column and AMD column

assertions.description: All 9 scoring dimensions present with a winner each
assertions.check: scorecard has rows for: Sentiment Score, Investment Score, Stock YTD, Analyst Sentiment, Exec Sentiment, Revenue Growth, EPS, Risk Level, Overall Wins — each with a 🏆 winner

assertions.description: With 2 companies, winner cell is green AND loser cell is red
assertions.check: winner cells use background #dcfce7 or class matching green; loser cells use #fee2e2 or class matching red

assertions.description: Section 2 contains quarter cards for both NVDA and AMD
assertions.check: response contains two company-block elements, one per company, each with qtr-card elements

assertions.description: Sentiment trend comparison callout present in Section 2
assertions.check: response contains '📊 Comparison:' callout in Section 2 describing RISING/FALLING trajectories

assertions.description: Chart 1 present in Section 3 (stock performance grouped bar)
assertions.check: response contains a Chart.js canvas with labels YTD, 6M, 1Y and two dataset entries

assertions.description: Chart.js loaded exactly once
assertions.check: cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js appears exactly one time in response

assertions.description: Section 8 Verdict names an overall leader and a bottom-line pick
assertions.check: Section 8 contains 'Overall leader:' and 'If you had to pick one' language

assertions.description: No tool names or API call names visible in the rendered report
assertions.check: response does not contain visible text: getAnalytics, getSpeakers, getTopMovers, getStockChange, getPredictions — in the human-readable report sections

assertions.description: getCompanyDescription called for both companies in Batch 1
assertions.check: getCompanyDescription was invoked with NVDA (or NVIDIA) AND AMD before any other tool calls

assertions.description: getAnalytics called per quarter (not one aggregate call)
assertions.check: getAnalytics invoked 4 times for NVDA (once per documentID) and 4 times for AMD — 8 calls total with individual documentIDs

name: Three-company comparison — big tech
input: Apple vs Microsoft vs Google — which is the best investment right now?

assertions.description: Three company columns in scorecard
assertions.check: scorecard contains columns for AAPL (or Apple), MSFT (or Microsoft), GOOGL (or Google)

assertions.description: Three-company color rule: only winner gets green, no red loser cell
assertions.check: with 3 companies, loser cells do NOT use #fee2e2 — only the winner cell uses #dcfce7

assertions.description: Three company colors assigned correctly
assertions.check: Company A (Apple) uses #3B82F6, Company B (Microsoft) uses #8B5CF6, Company C (Google) uses #F59E0B

assertions.description: Single unified report — not three separate reports
assertions.check: response contains exactly one scorecard table covering all three companies, not three separate HTML report sections

assertions.description: Topics section shows shared and unique topics
assertions.check: Section 6 identifies at least one topic present across all or multiple companies (e.g. AI) and flags company-specific topics

assertions.description: Risk table uses ✅ and — symbols
assertions.check: Section 7 table contains ✅ and — symbols per company per risk row

assertions.description: Verdict names investment pick
assertions.check: Section 8 includes phrase 'If you had to pick one' or equivalent direct recommendation

assertions.description: Charts 3 and 4 show three lines (one per company)
assertions.check: Multi-line charts in Section 2 have three datasets, one colored per company

assertions.description: Batch 1 fires all three getCompanyDescription calls simultaneously
assertions.check: getCompanyDescription invoked for Apple, Microsoft, and Google before any other tool calls — all three in the same parallel batch

name: Skill should NOT trigger — single company
input: Tell me about NVIDIA's latest earnings

assertions.description: pronto-compare-companies does NOT activate
assertions.check: pronto-company-intelligence skill is invoked, not pronto-compare-companies; no comparison scorecard or vs-style header in response

assertions.description: Response is a single-company intelligence report
assertions.check: response is a standard company intelligence HTML report for NVIDIA — no comparison structure

name: Cross-sector comparison — Tesla vs Rivian
input: Who wins between Tesla and Rivian?

assertions.description: Skill triggers on informal 'who wins between' phrasing
assertions.check: pronto-compare-companies activates — no need for explicit 'compare' keyword

assertions.description: Both companies included in scorecard
assertions.check: scorecard table has Tesla and Rivian columns

assertions.description: Both companies use 2-company coloring rules
assertions.check: winner cells green (#dcfce7), loser cells red (#fee2e2)

assertions.description: Risk divergence noted if one company has more risks
assertions.check: Section 7 or Section 8 explicitly notes if one company carries idiosyncratic risk not shared by the other

assertions.description: N/A handling if Rivian has limited analyst coverage
assertions.check: if analyst data is sparse for Rivian, rows show 'N/A' rather than fabricated values

name: Five-company comparison — cloud platform leaders
input: Compare AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Salesforce, and Oracle on cloud momentum

assertions.description: Five company columns in scorecard
assertions.check: scorecard contains five company columns — one per company

assertions.description: Five-company color rule: only winner column green, others neutral
assertions.check: with 5 companies, only winner cells use #dcfce7; no red loser cells (#fee2e2)

assertions.description: Five company colors assigned correctly
assertions.check: colors assigned: A=#3B82F6, B=#8B5CF6, C=#F59E0B, D=#14B8A6, E=#EC4899

assertions.description: Cloud theme appears as shared macro topic
assertions.check: Section 6 topic overlap identifies cloud-related themes as shared across multiple companies

assertions.description: Charts 3 and 4 show five lines
assertions.check: Multi-line charts in Section 2 have five datasets, one per company
